context: The mid-1960s saw Hindi cinema embracing a wider range of styles, from the twist dance craze (Manna Dey's 'Aao Twist Kare') to devotional songs and romantic ballads. Shankar-Jaikishan and Kalyanji-Anandji dominated as music directors. The rare appearance of classical vocalist Kishori Amonkar at #10 showcased the blurring of boundaries between classical and film music.

tracks.title: Jis Dil Me Basa Tha Pyar Tera

Tumhi Mere Mandir, Tumhi Meri Pooja
Aaja Aai Bahar, Dil Hai Beqraar
